misconceptionSummary: Students apply a rule without understanding why powers appear when variables are multiplied together.

visuals.ruleText: An algebraic term has a coefficient part and a variable part
visuals.subText: Both parts are involved when we multiply terms

steps.narration: An algebraic term is built from two pieces, the coefficient and the variable part. Multiplication affects both pieces.
When the variables are different, we keep both of them. Two a times four b becomes eight a b.
If the same variable appears more than once, the multiplication is repeated, so we write it as a power.
This also makes sense in context. If the side lengths are three x and two x, the area is six x squared.

visuals.expression: Multiplication combines the number parts and combines the variable parts
visuals.label: Repeated variables create powers

steps.narration: The important idea is structure. Multiplying terms combines what is numerical and combines what is algebraic.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: thinking x × x stays as x. Multiplying by x again changes the variable part and creates x squared.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The second x matters. It does not disappear. It turns one x into x squared.
